_comment: World B -- resolves abstract cascade roles to concrete rails PER HOST. Extends the merged kind->executor routing (manifest-schema.md) with a usage-aware fallback ladder. The ONLY file holding host-specific knowledge; keep all harness lock-in here. kind values: native = host runs it in-process (cascade-dispatch emits a directive, exit 64, the orchestrator dispatches the Claude/Codex subagent); codex_companion = resolve CODEX_ROOT (dual-cache) and run codex-companion.mjs task --write (matches execution-orchestrator.md 3d); wrapper = run openrouter-wrapper.sh; none = role unavailable on this host (skipped). probe = which usage-probe.sh rail to gauge. models = ordered list tried within the role (best first); for codex_companion it is representative-only (Codex uses its own configured model, no --model flag).
_detect: Resolution order: --host arg > $CASCADE_HOST env > auto. Auto: $CLAUDE_CODE/$CLAUDECODE => claude-code; $CODEX_SANDBOX/$CODEX_HOME => codex; else generic.

claude-code._comment: Cockpit = Claude Code on PRO (downgraded from Max, effective 2026-08). Codex Pro 20x via codex-companion.mjs is now the deep subscription rail -- prefer it for anything not needing Claude-native visual judgment. native_judgment is demoted to Pro-level acceptance: sonnet leads (the Pro daily driver); opus and fable are likely plan-unavailable on Pro and stay as inert upgrade-path entries -- RC-64 degrades an unavailable native directive to the next model in the list, and if the plan later carries them again, promote them back to the head. gpt-5.6-sol CANNOT appear in native_judgment here (Claude-only `kind: native`); it reaches this host via premium_sub and frontier_api. See docs/opus-4-8-tuning.md (Fable vs GPT-5.6 Sol).

codex._comment: Cockpit = Codex on the ChatGPT sub. GPT-5.6 Sol is the native model (requires codex-cli >= 0.144.x; 0.128.x rejects the 5.6 family with 'requires a newer version of Codex'). gpt-5.5 (rank 96) sits second in both native roles as the rung an older CLI can still reach. NOTE: cascade-dispatch.sh emits only the FIRST floor-clearing model and exit-64s; walking to gpt-5.5 is done by the orchestrator's Native Model Descent (execution-orchestrator.md, RC 64), which retries the next model in this list on a 'requires a newer version of Codex' error. Without that descent this rung would be decorative. gpt-5.1-codex-mini is deliberately ABSENT: it returns HTTP 400 'not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account', which is exactly this host's stated deployment. Claude only via API/OpenRouter. This host is where Sol leads native_judgment, because here `native` means OpenAI. premium_sub and native_judgment intentionally hold the same list: premium_sub serves the `codex` class, native_judgment serves the `claude` class (ui/integration) on this host, where no Claude native rail exists.

generic._comment: Neutral harness (opencode, Pi, etc.) with no subscription rails. Everything is API via the wrapper. premium_sub absent => skipped; judgment maps to a frontier API model.
